source.notes: Optimus _ Einride - 2026_03_13 12_58 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Discovery-and-demo call between Optimus (Ed Stockman, Max Riemer) and Adam Osborn of an asset-based EV/autonomous carrier who needs to find local freight within a 150-200 mile radius of his charging terminals (Chicago, Kernney NJ) to boost asset utilization and win backhauls. Max demoed new origin/destination radius filtering on Adam's actual lanes and shippers (Costco, Cisco), while Ed differentiated Optimus's funded/transacted, geocoded data from Genlogs's inferred data and candidly conceded the dataset underreports high-volume dedicated-carrier and private-fleet loads — Adam's central data-quality concern. The call ended with a trial plan (more credits or a paid profile) plus Adam cross-referencing against his own customer data, with a decision expected soon; Adam is weighing Optimus against Genlogs with budget for only one.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Ed was highly responsive and consultative — probing on competitors, engaging Adam's specific data-quality concern, and even steering him to Genlogs where it was a better fit; although his vocal energy faded and sat well below his own baseline (arousal z -4.7), his sharp questions and honest, tailored answers show genuine attentiveness, not scripted delivery.

evidence.quote: who would who would you bucket as our competitors for this?
if you're looking for something that's far more specialized like around private fleet data um you know I'd probably steer you towards Genox

value_prop_clarity.explanation: A neutral listener could summarize it: Optimus shows real, funded, geocoded shipper transactions with actual facility names and paperwork, versus competitors that infer pickup and delivery — so the lane data is precise, not assumed.

evidence.quote: these are these are integrated transactions that we have seen be funded that means transacted like an actual paid transaction
they're inferring and making assumptions about where it picks up and where it delivers to
we do not take any type of inferred data

relevance.explanation: The pitch was built around Adam's actual terminals (Chicago, Kernney), his 150-200 mile EV range constraint, and his asset-utilization use case, culminating in a head-to-head trial framed on his own parameters.

evidence.quote: Max, do you want to show the facility uh stuff as well specifically for Kernney?
if you're able to put in Kernney and 200 mile radius uh on both sides and um you're able to start, you know, making those cold calls

discovery_progression.explanation: Ed asked strong, productive questions throughout, but he never secured a specific dated next step — he asked the buyer to define the next move and left the follow-up cadence and decision timeline entirely buyer-driven, with only a loose 'regroup next week.'

evidence.quote: that's the more relevant data set or would you would you leverage two data sets?
Um where what do you think is the best next step?

Ed Stockman.coaching_action: Before ending the call, propose and lock a specific dated checkpoint with success criteria — e.g., 'let's hold 30 minutes on [date] to review your Kernney/Cisco cross-reference and any qualified-shipper calls' — instead of leaving the next step and decision timeline for the buyer to define.

delivery_engagement.explanation: In his demo-driver role Max was responsive and collaborative, adjusting filters to Adam's requests in real time and explicitly inviting course-correction; his acoustics sat right at his own baseline with the highest arousal of the three speakers.

evidence.quote: this is what I'm thinking and Ed or Adam sort of pop in if you thought think or are thinking differently of this
Cisco has nine matching facilities within a 50-mi radius of that area

value_prop_clarity.explanation: Max made the core value tangible by showing exactly how the new filter isolates matching lanes and cuts the noise that previously required manual map-clicking, backed by concrete counts (nine matching facilities).

evidence.quote: before we had the the matching lanes, it was we showed the top lanes and then you had to go click in the map to sort of find the network
Cisco has nine matching facilities within a 50-mi radius of that area

relevance.explanation: The entire demo used Adam's real geography and shippers — Chicago and Kernney terminals, enterprise names like Costco and Cisco, and the 50/200-mile radii that map to his EV range.

evidence.quote: going with let's just use Chicago as an example. And if we did enterprise, we'll throw large in there, too.
Cisco has nine matching facilities within a 50-mi radius of that area

discovery_progression.explanation: Max's questions were mostly demo-navigation rather than discovery, and while he owns the trial logistics, he committed only to an open-ended 'shoot you a note' and a loose 'week or so, then regroup' with no firm date.

evidence.quote: we just have you on there for like a week or so and then we can regroup after that

Max Riemer.coaching_action: During the demo, pause after each feature to confirm it actually solves the problem Adam raised — e.g., 'does this list view fix the aggregation issue you mentioned?' — turning the screen-share into two-way discovery rather than one-way narration.

signals.signal: Imminent, buyer-owned decision and self-initiated follow-up
signals.quote: We're going to make a decision pretty quick, so I'll keep you guys posted

signals.signal: Proactive plan to validate data quality fast against his own customer data
signals.quote: So I have their data sets I can cross reference and we can get there pretty quick to understand data quality

signals.signal: Positive ROI framing on price — objection-free reaction to the quote
signals.quote: 30, 40 grand, something like that, like that's not going to take long for us to to recoup if we land a large enterprise shipper

signals.signal: Material, unresolved objection: factoring-sourced data misses his highest-volume targets, and he is choosing between two vendors with budget for one
signals.quote: unlikely that they're all using factoring companies so that volume disappears
